dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini won because HN gave it two direct product-use wins: commenters in the NanoClaw SEO thread said Gemini and Google AI Mode found the real site and warned about impostors, and a separate discussion praised Gemini as a much better troubleshooting tool than sifting through ad-heavy tech articles, even while worrying about what that does to publishers [E3][E4].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini had the clearest combination of positive sentiment and repeated coverage. One front-page thread singled out Gemini/Google AI Mode as standout performers that both identified the official NanoClaw pages and explicitly warned about fake domains [E3]. Another thread described Gemini as materially more useful than conventional tech-search workflows for a real hardware task, with the main caveat aimed at the publishing economics around AI answers rather than the quality of Gemini's response itself [E4].

openai.judgementSnippet: HN gave OpenAI one small assist when a commenter said ChatGPT correctly surfaced the official NanoClaw site, but the dedicated GPT‑5.3 Instant thread was dominated by complaints about confusing model branding, degraded ChatGPT writing style, and awkward example choices [E2][E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: A single formal-methods discussion said Claude could produce Dafny code that was usable enough to review and experiment with, but it also missed proof obligations and the commenter ultimately preferred returning to simpler human-readable code paths [E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Commenters singled out Gemini and Google AI Mode for warning about fake NanoClaw sites and called them standouts among AI answers; another thread described Gemini as dramatically better than rummaging through tech articles for a PSU-cabling problem, though that praise came with concern that AI isn't compensating the authors it learns from [E3][E4].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: The clearest Copilot mention said it found the real NanoClaw website and GitHub repo, but it also surfaced a likely fake X account, so the discussion read as getting the main task mostly right while still being careless around adjacent links [E5].

evidence.summary: The GPT‑5.3 Instant launch thread skewed negative for OpenAI, with top comments criticizing confusing product naming, worsened ChatGPT tone/style, and unsettling examples rather than celebrating the release.
In the NanoClaw SEO discussion, a commenter reported that ChatGPT linked to the official website and repo when testing AI tools against fake project sites.
The NanoClaw SEO thread praised Google AI Mode and Gemini for identifying the official project pages and explicitly warning about fake domains, with one commenter calling them standout AI answers.

In a discussion about tech sites losing search traffic, one commenter said Gemini was much better than browsing articles for a real PSU-cabling problem, but also worried that tools like Gemini redirect value away from the original authors.
The NanoClaw SEO comparison said Copilot correctly returned the official website and GitHub repo, but also linked a likely fake X account, leaving a mixed impression.

In the software-verification thread, a commenter said Claude could generate reviewable Dafny code for a prototype, but it also failed to carry proofs far enough and did not eliminate the need for human judgment.
